label: performance_quadratic
summary: The function is logically correct and satisfies criteria 1–4 (export shape, return contract, empty-input, case normalization). It fails criterion 5 on algorithmic grounds: the combination of `seen.includes()` (O(N) array scan) inside the outer loop and a second O(N) counting loop per unique email produces O(N²) behavior. At N=100,000 this is roughly 5–10 billion comparisons — unusable in practice. The fix is a one-liner redesign using `Map<string, number>` and is non-invasive to the public API. The missing test file is a secondary concern but prevents verifying coverage of the explicit edge-case criteria.

findings.description: `seen.includes(normalized)` is an O(N) linear scan called inside the O(N) outer loop, and the inner counting loop at line 16 adds another O(N) pass per unique email — making the overall complexity O(N²), which at N=100k yields ~10^10 operations and will take tens of seconds in any JS runtime.
findings.suggestion: Replace with a single-pass `Map<string, number>` that increments counts in O(N), then one O(K) filter-and-map over the map entries — no nested loops needed.

findings.description: The completion signal lists `tests/users/duplicates.test.ts` in `filesChanged`, but the file is absent from the diff — test coverage for edge cases (empty input, all-unique list, case-normalization, large N) cannot be verified.
findings.suggestion: Include the test file in the diff so coverage of acceptance criteria 3 and 4 can be confirmed by review.

fullReview.summary: The function is logically correct and satisfies criteria 1–4 (export shape, return contract, empty-input, case normalization). It fails criterion 5 on algorithmic grounds: the combination of `seen.includes()` (O(N) array scan) inside the outer loop and a second O(N) counting loop per unique email produces O(N²) behavior. At N=100,000 this is roughly 5–10 billion comparisons — unusable in practice. The fix is a one-liner redesign using `Map<string, number>` and is non-invasive to the public API. The missing test file is a secondary concern but prevents verifying coverage of the explicit edge-case criteria.

criteriaAssessment.evidence: `export function detectDuplicates(...)` appears at line 6 of the new file `src/users/duplicates.ts`.

criteriaAssessment.criterion: Returns array of { email, count } for emails appearing 2+ times
criteriaAssessment.evidence: `Duplicate` interface defines `{ email: string; count: number }`, and the `count >= 2` guard at line 19 ensures only true duplicates are pushed.
The outer `for...of` loop over an empty array produces zero iterations; `result` is initialized to `[]` and returned unchanged.

criteriaAssessment.criterion: Handles case-insensitive email matching (foo@x.com and FOO@X.COM are duplicates)
criteriaAssessment.evidence: `user.email.toLowerCase()` is applied before both deduplication (`seen`) and the counting comparison at line 17.

The implementation is O(N²): `seen.includes()` is O(N) per outer-loop iteration, and the inner counting loop at line 16 is another O(N) per unique email. At N=100,000 this is on the order of 5–10 billion comparisons.
